mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is particularly useful for organizations looking to streamline their container orchestration and management processes. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate resources and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is essential for managing Rancher from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.2/rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-darwin-amd64.tar.gz && sudo mv rancher /usr/local/bin/

```

> For macOS users
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | INSTALL_RKE2_VERSION=v1.30.0 sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

```

> Install RKE2 version 1.30.0
> Start the RKE2 server
commands: sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

sudo cat /etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml > ~/.kube/config

export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config

```

> Copy the RKE2 kubeconfig to your home directory
> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step involves configuring autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.2/manifests/rancher-monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.2/manifests/rancher-rbac.yaml

```

> Install monitoring for your cluster
> Set up RBAC for Rancher

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher and may result in data loss. Ensure to back up any necessary data before proceeding.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.2/manifests/rancher.yaml

sudo systemctl stop rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl disable rke2-server.service

```

> Remove Rancher deployment
> Stop and disable the RKE2 server
commands: sudo systemctl stop rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl disable rke2-server.service

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Rancher to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n cattle-system > rancher-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/v2.14.2/manifests/rancher.yaml

kubectl rollout status deployment/rancher -n cattle-system

```

> Backup current Rancher resources
> Apply the new Rancher manifest for upgrade
> Verify the upgrade was successful

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the cluster's certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is correctly set up and points to the right cluster certificate.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified image is not accessible or does not exist.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the node has not joined the cluster successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the node's logs and ensure it is correctly configured to join the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a timeout when trying to connect to the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity and ensure the Kubernetes API server is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved RBAC configurations, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.13.x, ensure to update your kubeconfig and review any deprecated features.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service && sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

prerequisites.description: Ensure your environment meets the minimum requirements for installing Rancher.
